pattern: Game theory insight: Alice wins whenever the string has at least one vowel, because she can always use the odd-vowel removal to leave Bob in a losing position.
hints: Count the total number of vowels in s.
If there are zero vowels, Alice cannot make any move and loses immediately.
If there is at least one vowel, Alice can always find a move (an odd-vowel substring), so she wins.

edge_inputs.description: No vowels at all — Alice loses
Even number of vowels but still one vowel present — Alice wins
Two characters, one vowel and one consonant — Alice wins

stress.description: Random lowercase string of length up to 100000
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(string.ascii_lowercase) for _ in range(size))
    return [s]
